content.problemStatement:
Chefland is an undirected, unweighted tree on NNN nodes. The nodes are labeled from 111 to NNN.

Define:


pathmin(u,v)=\text{pathmin}(u,v) = pathmin(u,v)= the minimum label of a node on the simple path from uuu to vvv.
pathmax(u,v)=\text{pathmax}(u,v) = pathmax(u,v)= the maximum label of a node on the simple path from uuu to vvv.
cost(u,v)=pathmin(u,v)‚ãÖpathmax(u,v)\text{cost}(u,v) = \text{pathmin}(u,v)\cdot\text{pathmax}(u,v)cost(u,v)=pathmin(u,v)‚ãÖpathmax(u,v)
If Chef is currently at node uuu, he can move to node vvv in a single move by spending cost(u,v)\text{cost}(u,v)cost(u,v) coins.

Chef starts at node SSS. For each i=1,2,3,‚Ä¶,Ni = 1, 2, 3, \ldots, Ni=1,2,3,‚Ä¶,N, find the minimum number of coins that Chef needs to spend in order to reach node iii from SSS, using the given move any number of times.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and SSS ‚Äî the number of nodes in the tree and the node that Chef starts out on.
The next N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines describe the edges. The iii-th of these N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui‚Äã and viv_ivi‚Äã, denoting an edge between uiu_iui‚Äã and viv_ivi‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN space-separated integers, where the iii-th integer denotes the minimum number of coins that Chef needs to spend in order to reach node iii from node SSS.
constraints: The input graph describes a tree.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The first 333 optimal paths are as follows:

Reaching 1: Move from node 333 to node 111 for a cost of 1‚ãÖ3=31 \cdot 3 = 31‚ãÖ3=3.
Reaching 2: Move from node 333 to node 111 for a cost of 333 and then node 111 to node 222 for a cost of 222.
Reaching 3: Chef is already at 333.
